metadata.name: Apartment Layout Engine Assumptions v25
notes: v25 extends v24 with a progressive multi-pass solve orchestrator instead of relying on room/3D passes as isolated additions.
The orchestrator is coarse-to-fine, topology-first, geometry-later, selective-refinement, lazy-exactness, and bounded-repair.
v25 adds pass execution, trigger-based escalation, confidence stopping, dirty-region exactification, representative solving, and hotspot 3D gating.
v25 still does not claim a full arbitrary exact polygon or full-building exact 3D solver; those remain routed to v26.

policy_notes: APT CA2 is used for geometry/BOM-oriented inheritance when it adds vertical or support-space detail not explicit in APT CA1.
APT MT and Gensler MEP are not direct drivers of apartment geometry; they are cross-check sources for downstream demand factors.

override_policy.value_flow: base_value = formula or assumption; resolved_value = user_override if apply=true else base_value

utility_infrastructure_exterior.formula: required_kva = demand_kva*1.25*(1+load_growth_ratio); qty/rating = argmin(CEILING(required_kva/rating_kva,1)*selection_cost_usd); area = IF(NOT(customer_station_indoor_enabled) OR required_kva<=249, XLOOKUP(selected_rating, guide_by_type)*qty*(1+layout_growth_ratio) + other_area_sf, 0)

vertical_transport.shaft_story_gate_formula: passenger_shaft = IF(total_stories>1, calculated_passenger_qty, 0); freight_shaft = IF(total_stories>1, calculated_freight_qty, 0); stair_shaft = IF(total_stories>1, calculated_stair_qty, 0)
vertical_transport.wheelchair_lift_formula: IF(enabled AND total_stories<=1, ROUND(LERP(dwelling_units,100->1,1000->2),0) * MAX(landing_ewa_sf, landing_code_min_sf) * stop_count, 0)
vertical_transport.elevator_machine_room_formula: passenger_room = XLOOKUP(rated_load_lb_default, guide_by_type, room_sf); freight_room = IF(has_freight_elevator, freight_width_ft*freight_depth_ft, 0); total = IF(has_passenger_elevator OR has_freight_elevator, (passenger_room + freight_room) * IF(on_roof_default, 1, 2), 0)
vertical_transport.elevator_machine_room_split_formula: interior = IF(has_machine_room AND NOT(on_roof_default), total, 0); roof = IF(has_machine_room AND on_roof_default, total, 0)

plumbing.gas_meter_space_alcove_formula: IF(enabled AND NOT(gas_utility_room_enabled), (bank_depth_ft + front_clear_ft) * (IF(gas_meter_count<=1, single_bank_width_ft, IF(gas_meter_count=2, two_meter_bank_width_ft, two_meter_bank_width_ft + (gas_meter_count-2)*additional_meter_width_ft)) + side_clear_ft), 0)

space_catalog_defaults.space_name: Storage Room - Cold Storage Delivery Room
Trash - Trash Vestibules at Res Floors (Typical)
Fan Room / Air Handling Unit (AHU) Room
Mechanical Shaft / Vertical Riser Space - Commercial Kitchen
Mechanical Shaft / Vertical Riser Space - Ventilation & Ref. Lines

notes_v13: Candidate solving remains the first stable thread boundary.
Repo integration should not introduce nondeterministic ProjectDoc ordering; stable sort after threaded solve.
Future v14 may split floor-family repair and support-space demand into separate scoped thread pools.

notes: TypeScript remains the canonical ProjectDoc owner in the current GLOQSpaceAllocation contract.
ProjectDoc export is treated as a generated snapshot, not as a second domain owner.
Keep engine sidecar JSON next to exported ProjectDoc to avoid semantic loss.
v13 fixes the v12 ambiguity where below-grade and podium ranges could overlap in solver indexing.
At least one upper residential level must remain after below-grade and podium allocation.

doc_fields.name: Autolayout {case_group}
doc_fields.sitePlan: optional; edgeSetbacksFt currently defaults to uniform value

notes: Declared shape requires a pack the solver could not keep stable across allocation.
Typical floors in the same family diverge in gross or allocated area beyond the configured threshold.
Buildable floor coverage remains below target after residual conversion and local repair.
One or more space pairs still overlap beyond tolerance after local repair.
Overlap detection still uses a rect/polygon proxy instead of a fully robust polygon boolean kernel.
v16 retains every filled interval on each cross-section instead of collapsing immediately to a single max depth.
Split ratio and depth-break ratio are used as topology indicators for branched, courtyard, and non-convex floor plates.
v16 evaluates frontage runs as a graph-like spine/branch proxy without claiming to be a combinatorial exact graph solver.
Service reach is estimated along the inferred corridor detour ratio, not by final door-to-door routing.
v17 first decomposes each floor into stable topology segments before picking a shape-specific strategy.
The decomposition layer is shape-agnostic; it only reads interval-field transitions, frontage runs, and graph-like junction/cycle cues.
The common kernel remains shape-agnostic and only uses graph-like invariants extracted from the floor plate.
Shape-specific logic is applied only after the common kernel has produced a decomposition, capacities, and a morphology graph proxy.
Every specific strategy consumes the same common-kernel objects: stable segments, inferred graph, capacities, and v16 topology summaries.
v17 does not claim exact mixed-integer packing; the strategy layer is still schematic but far more explicit than the pre-v17 heuristic bundles.
Envelope construction in v18 begins from the actual site polygon, not from a bounding box surrogate.
Concave offsets, narrow necks, self-intersections, and slivers are repaired before topology decomposition proceeds.
The main v18 constructive stack uses constrained triangulation as the canonical polygon subdivision, then filters the dual graph by clearance and frontage opportunity.
Straight skeleton remains an optional experimental path, not the default runtime contract.
Corridor routing is no longer a single scalar length estimate; it is a graph construction problem over cell-graph nodes.
Loop repair is bounded and deterministic, and is used only when branch penalties or dead-end lengths exceed target bands.
V18 chooses band-graph + frontage-slot dynamic programming as the main constructive packing solver.
Rectangle packing is retained only as a local clipping primitive for some service spaces, not as the main polygon reasoning model.
Residual cells are explicitly classified as usable, service-convertible, mergeable, discardable, or trigger-repair.
Acute wedges and low-value pockets are converted to service uses first before being discarded.
V18 still uses adjacency proxies rather than full room-interior exact layouts, but wet-wall and shaft affinity are now constructive filters and repair triggers.
The shaft graph is a vertical continuity object; unreachable wet clusters are not merely soft penalties anymore.
Typical floors propagate locked core/shaft anchors first, then local floor-cell repacking occurs around them.
Podium-to-tower transfer remains local-first; a full multi-floor global solve is still deferred.
Search is bounded, deterministic, and lexicographically tie-broken.
Repair neighborhoods are intentionally local and inspectable; v18 does not adopt a fully global stochastic optimizer.

stack: shape interpretation with common-kernel + shape-pack conflict resolution
notes: v19 preserves the polygon-aware constructive substrate from v18.
v19 adds iterative convergence and invalidation rather than replacing the constructive substrate.
V18 converts topology from a score-only layer into a constructive cell-graph layer.
Legacy v15/v16/v17 scores remain useful as priors and tie-breaks, not as the primary constructor.
Entry, lobby, core, stair, and branch coverage are evaluated on the graph that the solver itself constructs.
The graph is separate from the evaluator so dead-end and detour issues can trigger repair moves.
Packing is performed over frontage slots and cell bands, then repaired locally with clip-and-merge moves.
Corner premium and accessible-unit variants are explicit modifiers inside the DP objective.
The repair engine is intentionally bounded and deterministic to stay inspectable in Rust and in repo-side diagnostics.
If a floor exhausts the iteration cap, v18 marks it for manual review rather than pretending the search converged.

topology_layer_reasoning.layer_0_geometry_normalization: Canonicalizes polygon input, resolves non-simple conditions, and preserves only contract-safe frontage tags. This layer is common and may hard-overwrite downstream geometry state.
topology_layer_reasoning.layer_1_morphology_sensing: Builds morphology signatures, interval samples, frontage runs, and morphology graph on top of canonical geometry. This layer is common and is allowed to re-sample after one shape-triggered restart.
topology_layer_reasoning.layer_2_shape_interpretation: Chooses declared/inferred/hybrid shape packs. This layer is shape-aware but cannot rewrite canonical geometry; it can only request bounded upstream restarts.
topology_layer_reasoning.layer_3_topology_zoning: Assigns habitable/circulation/service/void/residual pre-zones over the common kernel graph. This layer is common with shape-biased weights.
topology_layer_reasoning.layer_4_core_corridor_egress: Solves core/corridor/service graphs over zoning. Shape packs may bias family selection but routing validity remains common-kernel governed.
topology_layer_reasoning.layer_5_allocation_graph: Creates compatibility, adjacency, forbidden-adjacency, and typology run graphs used by constructive packing.
topology_layer_reasoning.layer_6_constructive_packing: Runs frontage-slot and cell-band assignment with coupled service/core/corridor placement.
topology_layer_reasoning.layer_7_local_repair: Applies bounded local repairs and decides whether to keep, rollback, or request targeted upstream invalidation.
topology_layer_reasoning.layer_8_vertical_consistency: Propagates stable states across floor families, transfer floors, and quarantined exceptions.
topology_layer_reasoning.layer_9_feasibility_contract: Collects unresolved contradictions into unclear zones and protects repo/project-doc ownership boundaries.

convergence_reasoning.stop_rule: Stop when hard conflicts are resolved or quarantined, weighted convergence gain drops below epsilon for two consecutive rounds, and no oscillation fingerprint is active.
convergence_reasoning.restart_rule: Restart locally when shape pack switches, core count changes, or routing invalidates zoning beyond budget. Restart globally only when oscillation persists after freeze rollback.

layer_1_to_2.allowed_feedback: shape mismatch can request one morphology resample

layer_2_to_3.allowed_feedback: routing/packing may request strategy fallback or hybridization

layer_3_to_4.allowed_feedback: routing may relabel service pockets and circulation bands

layer_4_to_5.allowed_feedback: packing may request corridor move or branch reduction

layer_5_to_6.allowed_feedback: repair may relabel compatibility weights and forbidden adjacencies

layer_6_to_7.allowed_feedback: repair is bounded and cannot rewrite geometry directly

layer_7_to_8.allowed_feedback: vertical layer may trigger sibling repair or quarantine

layer_8_to_9.allowed_feedback: feasibility can only emit warnings/manual-review/deferred routing

notes: All formulas remain schematic/pre-DD proxies.
These formulas are used for generator guidance, bounded search ranking, and unclear-zone routing; they are not permit-level structural analysis.

bar.grid_rule: align corridor spine with one primary support line; units should repeat by bay multiples along frontage runs.

l_shape.grid_rule: solve each wing on its own support axis and couple them at the elbow with a support-bridge node.

u_shape.grid_rule: stabilize two parallel wing grids before solving the court base bar.

o_shape.grid_rule: maintain ring continuity and avoid unsupported interior court corners.

h_shape.grid_rule: treat the connector as service/support biased unless two-bar stability is preserved.

tower.grid_rule: use compact core with sector bays; do not over-fragment the facade into off-module strips.

x_shape.grid_rule: the hub owns lateral stability; spokes inherit bay rhythm from the hub before local repair.

cluster.grid_rule: each lobe gets a local bay subgraph but the stack graph still tries to align shared shafts.

free_form.grid_rule: derive local grids per lobe from the common kernel and switch to hybridization when one global grid collapses.

perimeter_partial.grid_rule: stabilize the closed ring segment first, then solve the open edge as bar-like appendages.

opening_life_safety_reasoning.door_node_graph: Corridor graph is refined with unit-entry, stair-lobby, roof-access, and exterior-discharge door nodes before common-path and dead-end burden are evaluated.
opening_life_safety_reasoning.window_eligibility_proxy: Habitable frontage is only counted on edges that remain window-eligible after fire/no-window/service/mechanical filters are applied.
opening_life_safety_reasoning.non_goal: V21 is still not a final door-swing, hardware-set, smoke-control, or room-by-room operable-window legality checker.

material_strategy_reasoning.type_v: Shorter spans, stronger bearing-wall/corridor-wall bias, lower roof occupancy confidence, and stronger depth penalties on long wings.
material_strategy_reasoning.type_iii: Perimeter wall / rated wall bias with similar wing-depth caution but slightly higher noncombustible edge tolerance than type V.
material_strategy_reasoning.type_v_over_i: Podium-to-wood hybrid with transfer sensitivity, strong podium parking coupling, and rooftop amenity only if transfer and access remain stable.
material_strategy_reasoning.type_iii_over_i: Hybrid podium + type III upper stack with transfer-floor bias similar to type V over I but slightly stronger noncombustible perimeter preference.
material_strategy_reasoning.type_i: Column-slab / concrete-core friendly, larger span tolerance, better tower/roof/mechanical support, and weaker frontage penalties from columns when core is stable.

notes: Later stages inherit geometry labels, corridor/core anchors, and zone classifications from earlier stages.
Stage 3 exactification is only activated on flagged local clusters unless manual override requires more.
A later stage may only invalidate the immediately previous stage unless a major-break condition is met.
Roof/site/parking/open-space runs consume the frozen floor-stack state rather than reopening massing by default.
The engine should prefer fewer global passes with higher-quality inherited states over wide repeated search.
Exactification never reopens stage 0 directly; it escalates to stage 2 or a bounded full-floor repack only when required.

shape_family_allocation_reasoning.fallback_rule: allow common-kernel fallback only when declared shape is simple or hybrid coverage remains above threshold
notes: v23 turns shape coverage into an allocation-readiness gate rather than a score-only diagnostic.
A complex declared shape with frequent fallback is routed to unclear instead of being treated as silently acceptable.

allocation_completeness_reasoning.support_cluster_policy: support rooms may be clustered every N typical floors if the family remains vertically served
notes: v23 checks floor-role-based completeness before declaring a floor layout-ready.
The completeness gate is deterministic and sidecar-visible.

typical_floor_equivalence_reasoning.representative_floor_selection: choose the sibling floor with the lowest closure error and no overlap conflicts as representative
typical_floor_equivalence_reasoning.harmonization_policy: attempt local crop/fill on residual pockets before broad family repack
notes: v23 treats family_id as the first-class comparison key for typical-floor equivalence.
Transfer floors and quarantined exception floors are excluded from forced equality.

space_utilization_reasoning.utilization_policy: convert residuals to support/circulation before discarding as slivers
notes: v23 requires a floor-level area ledger so the solver can no longer hide unused space behind aggregate scores.
Coverage and closure are reported per floor and at the project aggregate level.

space_overlap_reasoning.remaining_overlap_routing: any overlap above tolerance after repair becomes postsolve unclear
notes: v23 enforces non-overlap as a sidecar-visible integrity gate.
Robust polygon boolean handling for arbitrary non-convex and curved cells remains deferred.

core_stack: snap to single tolerance grid before any boolean or overlap stage
remove duplicate and collinear vertices before topology reasoning
detect self-touch and pseudo-courtyard signatures before declaring polygon simple
prefer integer arrangement exactification for room and floor overlap checks
route non-canonical curves and unresolved self-touch to unclear zones
fast_paths: room-level partition by exact strip decomposition
slow_paths: manual review when curve semantics remain ambiguous

intent: carry arc/radial semantics separately from plain polygon snapshots
convert curves to deterministic chordized rings before arrangement build
snap radial sectors to shared local axes when possible
defer_policy: unsupported spline/nurbs remains non-canonical in v24
aggressive polygonization allowed only for sidecar analysis, not legal green-light

vertical_features: mezzanine bounded by depth ratio and head-height reserve
duplex and skip-stop couplings on same family stacks
shaft reserve volumes spanning served floors
slab openings for shafts, stairs, and inter-floor unit links
limits: exact structural transfer slab geometry still deferred
exact smoke/egress coupling for double-height atria still deferred
exact beam/duct collision in 3D remains bounded-proxy in v24

principles: same semantics across profiles; depth differs, meaning does not

performance_profile_reasoning.fast_preview: P0-P3 mandatory, P4 representative-only, no P10.
performance_profile_reasoning.balanced: P0-P8 default, P9 selective, P10 hotspot-only when triggered.
performance_profile_reasoning.deep_validation: P0-P10 on triggered zones with stronger local exact subset.
performance_profile_reasoning.research: same semantics as deep_validation but wider beams and larger budgets.

orchestrator_module_architecture.state_policy: immutable pass snapshots with explicit diff, dirty-region invalidation, and bounded candidate beams
orchestrator_module_architecture.trace_policy: every stop, escalation, reject, repair, and invalidation must be sidecar-visible
